plans.label: Baseline (Standard Emergency Protocol)
plans.description: EOC activated per standard Tier 1 emergency protocols. SFPD and SFFD on elevated readiness. Backup power only at EOC and hospitals per pre-existing agreements. No pre-positioned fuel convoys or enhanced agency coordination. Relies on PG&E self-directed restoration and normal supply chain recovery. Minimal active command coordination across agencies.

plans.label: Utility Restoration Priority
plans.description: PG&E restoration crews pre-deployed at Sunset Substation and 3 key distribution switching stations. Mobile generators staged in SoMa and Civic Center for cell tower priority. Real-time outage telemetry active across 5 zones allows rapid load re-routing. Estimated restoration timeline reduced by 40%. City contracts emergency transformer transport from Fresno (vs. waiting for PG&E regional). Cell and water pump power prioritized to slow information vacuum.

plans.label: Hospital & Critical Infrastructure First
plans.description: UCSF Mission Bay and Zuckerberg SF General guaranteed continuous fuel through city contracts — bypassing PG&E queue. Medical supply trucks with police escort pre-positioned. Ambulance services given priority fuel allocation. Regional blood center at Potrero Hill on protected generator circuit. ICU and OR operations fully protected at all 3 major hospitals. EOC coordinates DPH surge capacity. Banking infrastructure in Downtown restored early to enable capital access for emergency purchases.

plans.label: BART & Muni Restoration Priority
plans.description: Emergency BART transformer procurement from regional stockpile (Caltrans facility, Sacramento) — 18-hour lead time vs 36-hour standard. Muni buses prioritized with city fuel contracts. Emergency shuttle services on Bay Bridge replacing BART. Commuter trunk routes restored first, then neighborhood access. I-80 emergency lane cleared for supply convoy priority. Convoy routing reduces food/medicine delivery delay from 8 hours to 3 hours. Enables hospital supply runs and food store restocking.

plans.label: Distributed Mutual Aid Network
plans.description: No heavy enforcement posture. Municipal resources directed to community leaders: Mission, SoMa, Sunset neighborhood coordinators receive city backing, funding, and radio access. Food distribution hubs established at community centers with city-supplied food reserves. Senior welfare check program coordinated through Nextdoor and SF311. Water distribution trucks dispatched to Sunset and Richmond by community groups. Minimal SFPD visibility. Community resilience maximized — trust in government preserved. Slower infrastructure restoration but higher public cooperation.

plans.label: Full Cross-Agency Unified Command
plans.description: SF EOC activates Unified Command with PG&E, SFPD, SFFD, BART, Muni, DPH, DMH, DSW, Red Cross, and National Guard logistics. Real-time resource tracking dashboard active. Pre-positioned emergency supplies at 6 distribution points. Utility restoration + hospital priority + transit recovery + mutual aid all coordinated simultaneously. Requires all agencies to pre-agree protocols. Maximum resilience outcome. High communication burden — only viable if agencies trained together monthly.
